{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Office Assistant",
  "description": "Jumbo Health and Wellness Limited (Jumbo Sports) is a leading Nigerian provider of fitness, sports, and wellness solutions. Since 1998, we have specialized in the supply, installation, and maintenance of premium gym equipment, sports equipment, wellness products, and related solutions for individuals, corporate organizations, hospitality businesses, educational institutions, and government agencies. With a commitment to quality, innovation, and exceptional customer service, we help our clients create healthier and more active lifestyles. At Jumbo Sports, we are passionate about developing talented professionals and fostering a collaborative, performance-driven workplace where employees can grow and make meaningful contributions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Office Assistant

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos

Job Summary


	Jumbo Health &amp; Wellness Limited is looking for a reliable, hardworking, and physically fit Office Assistant to support the smooth running of our daily office operations.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for running official errands, maintaining a clean and organized work environment, assisting with the movement and setup of gym equipment, and providing general administrative and operational support across departments.


Key Responsibilities


	Run official office errands promptly and efficiently.
	Assist with loading, unloading, lifting, moving, and arranging gym equipment and other company assets.
	Support the warehouse, logistics, and technical teams during deliveries, installations, and dispatches.
	Maintain cleanliness and orderliness of the office, showroom, warehouse, and other work areas.
	Deliver documents, parcels, and other official items to internal and external locations.
	Monitor office supplies and notify the appropriate department when replenishment is required.
	Welcome visitors professionally and direct them to the appropriate personnel.
	Ensure company property and equipment are handled with care to prevent damage.
	Support stock movement and inventory-related activities when required.
	Adhere to all company health, safety, and operational procedures.
	Perform any other duties assigned by Management.


Requirements


	Minimum of SSCE or its equivalent.
	Previous experience in a similar role is an added advantage but not mandatory.
	Physically fit and capable of lifting and moving moderately heavy gym and fitness equipment.
	Good verbal communication and interpersonal skills.
	Honest, responsible, and dependable with a high level of integrity.
	Ability to follow instructions and work with minimal supervision.
	Strong organizational and time management skills.
	Willingness to learn and take on additional responsibilities.
	Resides within Lagos or nearby locations.


Key Competencies:


	Reliability and accountability
	Physical stamina
	Teamwork and collaboration
	Strong work ethic
	Attention to detail
	Integrity and professionalism
	Flexibility and adaptability
	Positive attitude


What We Offer


	Competitive salary and benefits.
	A supportive and professional work environment.
	The opportunity to work with one of Nigeria&#39;s leading health, wellness, and fitness equipment companies.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to: hrjumbo97@gmail.com, using &quot;Office Assistant&quot; as the subject of the email.

Note: Open until the position is filled.",

Office Assistant Job at Jumbo Health and Wellness Limited




        
    

        
        Jumbo Health and Wellness Limited (Jumbo Sports) is a leading Nigerian provider of fitness, sports, and wellness solutions. Since 1998, we have specialized in the supply, installation, and maintenance of premium gym equipment, sports equipment, wellness products, and related solutions for individuals, corporate organizations, hospitality businesses, educational institutions, and government agencies. With a commitment to quality, innovation, and exceptional customer service, we help our clients create healthier and more active lifestyles. At Jumbo Sports, we are passionate about developing talented professionals and fostering a collaborative, performance-driven workplace where employees can grow and make meaningful contributions.
     

            
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Office Assistant

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos

Job Summary


	Jumbo Health & Wellness Limited is looking for a reliable, hardworking, and physically fit Office Assistant to support the smooth running of our daily office operations.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for running official errands, maintaining a clean and organized work environment, assisting with the movement and setup of gym equipment, and providing general administrative and operational support across departments.


Key Responsibilities


	Run official office errands promptly and efficiently.
	Assist with loading, unloading, lifting, moving, and arranging gym equipment and other company assets.
	Support the warehouse, logistics, and technical teams during deliveries, installations, and dispatches.
	Maintain cleanliness and orderliness of the office, showroom, warehouse, and other work areas.
	Deliver documents, parcels, and other official items to internal and external locations.
	Monitor office supplies and notify the appropriate department when replenishment is required.
	Welcome visitors professionally and direct them to the appropriate personnel.
	Ensure company property and equipment are handled with care to prevent damage.
	Support stock movement and inventory-related activities when required.
	Adhere to all company health, safety, and operational procedures.
	Perform any other duties assigned by Management.


Requirements


	Minimum of SSCE or its equivalent.
	Previous experience in a similar role is an added advantage but not mandatory.
	Physically fit and capable of lifting and moving moderately heavy gym and fitness equipment.
	Good verbal communication and interpersonal skills.
	Honest, responsible, and dependable with a high level of integrity.
	Ability to follow instructions and work with minimal supervision.
	Strong organizational and time management skills.
	Willingness to learn and take on additional responsibilities.
	Resides within Lagos or nearby locations.


Key Competencies:


	Reliability and accountability
	Physical stamina
	Teamwork and collaboration
	Strong work ethic
	Attention to detail
	Integrity and professionalism
	Flexibility and adaptability
	Positive attitude


What We Offer


	Competitive salary and benefits.
	A supportive and professional work environment.
	The opportunity to work with one of Nigeria's leading health, wellness, and fitness equipment companies.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to: [email protected], using "Office Assistant" as the subject of the email.

Note: Open until the position is filled.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Electrical Engineer",
  "description": "Mikano International Limited is a Nigerian company in the power generation, real estate and construction sectors. This company offers sales, maintenance, assembly and distribution of power generation products, real estate development, and sales of construction equipment.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Electrical Engineer
Job Location: Ogba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Engineer is responsible for the installation, commissioning, maintenance, troubleshooting, and repair of diesel generators ranging from 20kVA and above across client sites, ensures reliable power supply, compliance with safety standards, and minimal downtime.


Key Responsibilities


	Install, commission, and test diesel generators (20kVA&ndash;500kVA)
	Carry out preventive and corrective maintenance.
	Diagnose and repair electrical faults in generators, control panels, ATS/AMF systems.
	Perform load testing, performance checks, and system inspections
	Maintain accurate service and maintenance records.
	Ensure compliance with HSE, company policies, and Nigerian electrical standards.
	Provide technical support to clients and respond to emergency breakdowns.
	Work closely with mechanical engineers and field technicians.


Requirements


	Trade Test Certificate, OND, HND, or B.Sc. in Electrical Engineering, or a related technical discipline.
	Minimum of 3 years of hands-on experience working with a reputable generator servicing or power solutions company.
	Strong knowledge of diesel generators, engines, and mechanical systems.
	Experience in field service engineering is an added advantage.
	Excellent troubleshooting and fault-diagnosis skills.
	Good oral and written communication skills.
	Strong reporting and documentation skills.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
	High level of professionalism, integrity, and attention to detail.


Benefits


	Transport Allowance
	Feeding Allowance
	Leave Allowance
	Health Maintenance Organization (HMO)
	Pension Scheme.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should kindly send their CV to: careers@mikano.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Electrical Engineer Job at Mikano International Limited




        
    

        
        Mikano International Limited is a Nigerian company in the power generation, real estate and construction sectors. This company offers sales, maintenance, assembly and distribution of power generation products, real estate development, and sales of construction equipment.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Electrical Engineer
Job Location: Ogba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Engineer is responsible for the installation, commissioning, maintenance, troubleshooting, and repair of diesel generators ranging from 20kVA and above across client sites, ensures reliable power supply, compliance with safety standards, and minimal downtime.


Key Responsibilities


	Install, commission, and test diesel generators (20kVA–500kVA)
	Carry out preventive and corrective maintenance.
	Diagnose and repair electrical faults in generators, control panels, ATS/AMF systems.
	Perform load testing, performance checks, and system inspections
	Maintain accurate service and maintenance records.
	Ensure compliance with HSE, company policies, and Nigerian electrical standards.
	Provide technical support to clients and respond to emergency breakdowns.
	Work closely with mechanical engineers and field technicians.


Requirements


	Trade Test Certificate, OND, HND, or B.Sc. in Electrical Engineering, or a related technical discipline.
	Minimum of 3 years of hands-on experience working with a reputable generator servicing or power solutions company.
	Strong knowledge of diesel generators, engines, and mechanical systems.
	Experience in field service engineering is an added advantage.
	Excellent troubleshooting and fault-diagnosis skills.
	Good oral and written communication skills.
	Strong reporting and documentation skills.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
	High level of professionalism, integrity, and attention to detail.


Benefits


	Transport Allowance
	Feeding Allowance
	Leave Allowance
	Health Maintenance Organization (HMO)
	Pension Scheme.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should kindly send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Quality Control Officer",
  "description": "Coleman Technical Industries Limited, the manufacturer of the reputable Coleman Wires and Cables, is Sub-the Saharan Africa&rsquo;s largest cable manufacturer, renowned for quality, reliability, and innovation. From its ultra-modern manufacturing facilities in Arepo and Sagamu, including its recently commissioned state-of-the-art plant, producing the best fibre-optic cables with FRP in Africa, the company remains committed to industrial excellence, safety, and national development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control Officer
Job Location: Arepo, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Quality Control

Job Summary


	We are seeking a Quality Control Officer to ensure all products meet industry standards, regulatory requirements, and company quality benchmarks.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for inspecting raw materials, monitoring production, conducting tests, and maintaining quality records to uphold product excellence.


Key Responsibilities
Quality Assurance &amp; Inspection:


	Conduct rigorous inspections of raw materials (e.g., copper, aluminum, insulation materials) to verify compliance with company specifications.
	Monitor every stage of cable production, identifying and addressing deviations from standard operating procedures.
	Perform random and routine quality checks on semi-finished and finished cables to ensure compliance with internal and external standards.
	Implement sampling procedures and statistical analysis to assess production quality.


Testing &amp; Compliance:


	Conduct mechanical, electrical, and environmental tests on low and high-voltage cables, including:
	Conductor resistance testing
	Insulation resistance testing
	High-voltage withstand testing
	Flame retardancy and fire resistance testing
	Tensile strength and elongation tests
	Ensure that all testing is in compliance with IEC, NIS, SON, and ISO 9001 standards.
	Work closely with the standards and certification teams to maintain regulatory compliance.
	Investigate and resolve customer complaints related to quality issues and ensure corrective actions are implemented.


Process Improvement &amp; Reporting:


	Identify and implement continuous improvement initiatives in manufacturing processes to enhance quality and reduce defects.
	Develop and enforce Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for quality assurance and control.
	Maintain comprehensive documentation of all inspections, test results, and quality incidents.
	Prepare and present detailed reports on quality performance metrics to senior management.
	Conduct root cause analysis (RCA) and work with production teams to implement corrective and preventive actions (CAPA).


Supplier &amp; Material Quality Management:


	Evaluate and approve new suppliers and materials to ensure they meet company quality standards.
	Conduct supplier audits and work with vendors to address material quality issues.
	Ensure traceability of materials, from procurement to production and final product delivery.


Health, Safety, and Environmental Compliance:


	Ensure all quality control processes align with safety regulations and environmental policies.
	Work closely with the Health &amp; Safety team to maintain a safe working environment.
	Participate in safety audits, risk assessments, and compliance training sessions.


Training &amp; Development:


	Provide training sessions for production staff on quality standards, best practices, and defect prevention.
	Stay up to date with industry advancements and recommend improvements in testing methodologies.
	Assist in the development of quality control manuals and training materials.


Qualifications &amp; Experience
Educational Requirements:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Industrial Chemistry, Polymer Science, or a related technical field.
	Additional certifications in Quality Control, ISO 9001, Six Sigma, or Total Quality Management (TQM) are a plus.


Experience:


	1-5 years of experience in a quality control role, preferably in a cable manufacturing or electrical components industry.
	Hands-on experience with cable testing equipment (e.g., high-voltage testers, tensile testing machines, insulation resistance meters).
	Strong knowledge of cable manufacturing processes, including extrusion, stranding, and sheathing.


Technical Skills:


	Proficiency in quality control tools (e.g., Statistical Process Control, Failure Mode and Effects Analysis, Root Cause Analysis).
	Ability to interpret engineering drawings, production blueprints, and technical specifications.
	Familiarity with local and international cable manufacturing standards (IEC, NIS, SON, ISO 9001).
	Soft Skills
	Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
	High attention to detail and accuracy.
	Excellent communication and teamwork skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet strict deadlines.


Others:


	Age: 25 years and above.
	Male candidates only.


Why Join Us?


	Work with a leading manufacturer of cables in Nigeria. And the largest in West Africa.
	Career growth and professional development opportunities.
	A collaborative and innovative work environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume to: careers@colemancables.com using &quot;Quality Control Officer&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Quality Control Officer Job at Coleman Technical Industries Limited




        
    

        
        Coleman Technical Industries Limited, the manufacturer of the reputable Coleman Wires and Cables, is Sub-the Saharan Africa’s largest cable manufacturer, renowned for quality, reliability, and innovation. From its ultra-modern manufacturing facilities in Arepo and Sagamu, including its recently commissioned state-of-the-art plant, producing the best fibre-optic cables with FRP in Africa, the company remains committed to industrial excellence, safety, and national development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control Officer
Job Location: Arepo, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Quality Control

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking a Quality Control Officer to ensure all products meet industry standards, regulatory requirements, and company quality benchmarks.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for inspecting raw materials, monitoring production, conducting tests, and maintaining quality records to uphold product excellence.


Key Responsibilities
Quality Assurance & Inspection:


	Conduct rigorous inspections of raw materials (e.g., copper, aluminum, insulation materials) to verify compliance with company specifications.
	Monitor every stage of cable production, identifying and addressing deviations from standard operating procedures.
	Perform random and routine quality checks on semi-finished and finished cables to ensure compliance with internal and external standards.
	Implement sampling procedures and statistical analysis to assess production quality.


Testing & Compliance:


	Conduct mechanical, electrical, and environmental tests on low and high-voltage cables, including:
	Conductor resistance testing
	Insulation resistance testing
	High-voltage withstand testing
	Flame retardancy and fire resistance testing
	Tensile strength and elongation tests
	Ensure that all testing is in compliance with IEC, NIS, SON, and ISO 9001 standards.
	Work closely with the standards and certification teams to maintain regulatory compliance.
	Investigate and resolve customer complaints related to quality issues and ensure corrective actions are implemented.


Process Improvement & Reporting:


	Identify and implement continuous improvement initiatives in manufacturing processes to enhance quality and reduce defects.
	Develop and enforce Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for quality assurance and control.
	Maintain comprehensive documentation of all inspections, test results, and quality incidents.
	Prepare and present detailed reports on quality performance metrics to senior management.
	Conduct root cause analysis (RCA) and work with production teams to implement corrective and preventive actions (CAPA).


Supplier & Material Quality Management:


	Evaluate and approve new suppliers and materials to ensure they meet company quality standards.
	Conduct supplier audits and work with vendors to address material quality issues.
	Ensure traceability of materials, from procurement to production and final product delivery.


Health, Safety, and Environmental Compliance:


	Ensure all quality control processes align with safety regulations and environmental policies.
	Work closely with the Health & Safety team to maintain a safe working environment.
	Participate in safety audits, risk assessments, and compliance training sessions.


Training & Development:


	Provide training sessions for production staff on quality standards, best practices, and defect prevention.
	Stay up to date with industry advancements and recommend improvements in testing methodologies.
	Assist in the development of quality control manuals and training materials.


Qualifications & Experience
Educational Requirements:


	Bachelor’s Degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Industrial Chemistry, Polymer Science, or a related technical field.
	Additional certifications in Quality Control, ISO 9001, Six Sigma, or Total Quality Management (TQM) are a plus.


Experience:


	1-5 years of experience in a quality control role, preferably in a cable manufacturing or electrical components industry.
	Hands-on experience with cable testing equipment (e.g., high-voltage testers, tensile testing machines, insulation resistance meters).
	Strong knowledge of cable manufacturing processes, including extrusion, stranding, and sheathing.


Technical Skills:


	Proficiency in quality control tools (e.g., Statistical Process Control, Failure Mode and Effects Analysis, Root Cause Analysis).
	Ability to interpret engineering drawings, production blueprints, and technical specifications.
	Familiarity with local and international cable manufacturing standards (IEC, NIS, SON, ISO 9001).
	Soft Skills
	Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
	High attention to detail and accuracy.
	Excellent communication and teamwork skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet strict deadlines.


Others:


	Age: 25 years and above.
	Male candidates only.


Why Join Us?


	Work with a leading manufacturer of cables in Nigeria. And the largest in West Africa.
	Career growth and professional development opportunities.
	A collaborative and innovative work environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume to: [email protected] using "Quality Control Officer" as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Data Entry / Store Officer",
  "description": "Coleman Technical Industries Limited, the manufacturer of the reputable Coleman Wires and Cables, is Sub-the Saharan Africa&rsquo;s largest cable manufacturer, renowned for quality, reliability, and innovation. From its ultra-modern manufacturing facilities in Arepo and Sagamu, including its recently commissioned state-of-the-art plant, producing the best fibre-optic cables with FRP in Africa, the company remains committed to industrial excellence, safety, and national development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Data Entry / Store Officer

Job Location: Deidei, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Stores / Warehouse

Job Summary


	Coleman Wires and Cables, the largest cable manufacturer in West Africa, is seeking a Store Officer to manage the receipt, storage, and issuance of raw materials, tools, and finished products in the warehouse and production store.
	The ideal candidate will ensure proper inventory control, stock accuracy, and efficient store operations to support manufacturing and supply chain processes.


Key Responsibilities
Inventory Management &amp; Stock Control:


	Maintain accurate records of stock levels, material usage, and product movement in the store.
	Conduct regular stock checks, cycle counts, and reconciliation of physical stock with ERP records.
	Ensure proper documentation of goods received, stored, and issued in line with company policies.
	Monitor minimum and maximum stock levels to prevent overstocking or stockouts.


Store Operations &amp; Material Handling:


	Oversee the receiving, storage, and dispatching of raw materials, consumables, and finished goods.
	Ensure proper arrangement and labeling of materials for easy identification and retrieval.
	Implement FIFO (First-In, First-Out) and LIFO (Last-In, First-Out) inventory methods where applicable.
	Supervise loading and offloading activities to minimize material handling damage.


ERP Data Entry &amp; Reporting:


	Update stock records in the ERP system for accurate tracking of inventory.
	Generate stock movement reports, material usage reports, and warehouse activity reports.
	Assist in inventory audits and ensure compliance with company stock management procedures.


Compliance &amp; Safety:


	Ensure the store is clean, organized, and meets safety regulations.
	Follow company health, safety, and environmental (HSE) guidelines in material handling.
	Monitor storage conditions of sensitive materials to prevent damage or deterioration.


Coordination &amp; Communication:


	Work closely with Production, Procurement, and Logistics teams to ensure smooth operations.
	Coordinate with suppliers and transport teams for timely stock replenishment and dispatch.
	Assist in training store personnel on inventory handling procedures.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Management or Social Sciences or a related field.
	3 - 5 years of experience in data entry, inventory or warehouse management in a manufacturing or industrial environment.
	Additional certifications in Inventory Management, Warehouse Operations, or ERP systems are an advantage.
	Experience working with ERP software for inventory management (SAP, Microsoft Dynamics, Odoo, or Oracle ERP).
	Applicant must be between 25 - 35 years old.


Technical Skills:


	Strong knowledge of warehouse management, inventory control, and stock reconciliation.
	Ability to use ERP systems, Microsoft Excel, and stock monitoring tools.
	Understanding of material handling, storage procedures, and supply chain best practices.


Soft Skills:


	High attention to detail and accuracy in stock management.
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities.
	Good teamwork and communication skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.


Why Join Us?


	Work with West Africa&rsquo;s largest cable manufacturer.
	Career growth and professional development opportunities.
	A structured and technology-driven work environment.
	Competitive salary and benefits package.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume to: careers@colemancables.com using &quot;Data Entry/Store Officer&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Second Engineer Job at Ocean Marine Solutions Limited




        
    

        
        Ocean Marine Solutions Limited is a strategically positioned organization operating the marine industry to provide solutions to high-risks security concerns in Nigerian waters. 

We are  recruiting candidaters to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Second Engineer

Job Location: Lagos
Job Type: Full time

Requirements
     

            
    




	Interested applicant(s) should possess relevant qualifications and experience.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
